Section 1
Sidekick Overview
03
Sidekick Overview

An agentic interface for IntentCX

  • Agentic, interact in natural languageYou work through an agent, not the UI.
  • Abstracts implementation detailsNo need to know prompting, which routine to edit, or what's active.
  • General-purpose and specific skillsA capable agent, plus skills tuned to our work.
04
Sidekick Overview

Connected to the tools you already use

Distillery

Build, test, and deploy agents.

Weave

Read production traces.

Jira

Features and issues.

Databricks

Production data & analysis.

Sidekick
05
Sidekick Overview

Skills encode repeatable workflows

Build

  • Edit a routine
  • Create evals
  • Build a feature

Fix

  • RCA a defect
  • Reproduce an issue
  • Fix an issue

Monitor

  • Run prod analysis
  • Pull eval results
  • Query Databricks

Section 3
Development Process
08
Development Process

Sidekick accelerates iteration velocity

  • Everyone brings a unique perspective to the agents we build for our customers — lowers the barrier to contribute.
  • IntentCX can only continue to scale at the rate we're moving if we empower more people to build → AI Everywhere.
  • Development teams no longer have to gate how much can be built.
This is already happeningDesign built an entire feature end to end with Sidekick for Billing chat's 11.8 release.
Development Process

But doing this safely matters more than ever

10
Development Process

Guardrails are built into Distillery

Branching

Edits happen on a branch, never directly on what's live.

Versioning

Every change is a saved version you can roll back to.

Evals

Automated tests confirm the agent still behaves before it ships.

Deployment

Merging doesn't go live instantly — changes ship through a deploy process.

Development Process

Updates must be tracked, tested, and reviewed

Jira ticket
→
Proposal
→
Evals
→
Approval
Development Process

Reviews should match the change's scope

Contained
  • Wording tweaks
  • Removing conflicting instructions
  • Reinforcing guidance
Review path

Asynchronous

Pod's dev lead or PO approves proposal

Structural
  • Behavior changes
  • New flows
  • Materially changing the end-user experience
Review path

Synchronous

Changes reviewed by product + dev + design

Pod's dev lead or PO approves proposal
